title: SEEGNAL INC. ANNOUNCES FILING OF 2025 ANNUAL FILINGS AND THAT THE MCTO WILL NO LONGER BE IN EFFECT AS OF MAY 16, 2026

title: Ipsen presents first-in-class late-breaking Phase II corabotase data in glabellar lines showing sustained duration of effect reinforced by consistently high patient satisfaction
description: PARIS, FRANCE, 16 May 2026 – Ipsen (Euronext: IPN; ADR: IPSEY) today presented the first corabotase data (n=183) for moderate-to- severe glabellar lines at the 2026 Scale Symposium in Nashville, TN. Corabotase is Ipsen’s first-in-class recombinant neuroinhibitor, RNITM. Built from engineered functional domains of A (catalytic) and B (binding), every element of its structure has been deliberately optimized to increase receptor affinity, enhance uptake, and improve resistance

title: Ipsen présente les derniers résultats d'une étude de phase II sur la corabotase, première de sa classe, concernant les rides glabellaires, montrant une durée d'effet prolongée et un niveau de satisfaction constamment élevé chez les patients
description: PARIS, FRANCE, 16 mai 2026 – Ipsen (Euronext: IPN; ADR: IPSEY) a présenté aujourd’hui les premières données sur la corabotase (n = 183) concernant les rides glabellaires modérées à sévères lors du symposium Scale 2026 à Nashville, dans le Tennessee. La corabotase est le premier neuro-inhibiteur recombinant (RNITM) de sa catégorie développé par Ipsen. title: NASA releases final RFP for Mars communications orbiter
description: NASA has released the final request for proposals for a Mars telecommunications system, confirming requirements that limit the companies that can bid on it.

title: York Space defends growth strategy as SDA programs are reshaped
description: Company executives acknowledge ‘investor confusion’ as Space Force reorganizes York’s primary customer, the Space Development Agency

title: Space Force awards Northrop Grumman $398 million satellite contract
description: The company will build a communications satellite to test protected communications technologies for contested environments

title: SpaceX launches CRS-34 cargo mission to ISS
description: A Falcon 9 launched a Dragon cargo spacecraft May 15 carrying nearly 3,000 kilograms of cargo to the International Space Station.

title: Starlink Mobile challengers back proposed telco-led D2D joint venture
description: A planned direct-to-device joint venture between AT&T, T-Mobile and Verizon has divided operators behind the satellites needed to connect ordinary smartphones from space.

title: Joint ESA-China SMILE mission set for launch to study Earth’s magnetic shield
description: A pioneering joint ESA-China science mission to study Earth’s magnetosphere is set to launch May 19, after a decade of preparations.

title: Virgin Galactic reaffirms plans to begin commercial service this year
description: Virgin Galactic said May 14 it remains on track, technically and financially, to start commercial flights of its next-generation suborbital spaceplane before the end of the year.

title: Chinese satellite maker MinoSpace seeks $736 million in IPO
description: Chinese satellite maker MinoSpace has seen its initial public offering application accepted, seeking $736 million for constellation and product expansion plans.

title: Cowboy files plans for up to 20,000 orbital data centers
description: Cowboy Space has filed plans with the Federal Communications Commission for a 20,000-satellite “Stampede” orbital data center constellation, shortly after raising $275 million to develop rockets whose upper stages would serve as the computing platforms.

title: Intuitive Machines to buy ground station company
description: Intuitive Machines has agreed to purchase a company that operates ground stations in the United States and United Kingdom to help build out a lunar communications network.

title: Golden Dome chief pushes back on $1.2 trillion CBO estimate
description: Gen. Michael Guetlein defends plan to pursue space-based interceptors but warns affordability remains central challenge

title: Iridium to take over Aireon to expand aviation safety business
description: Iridium Communications is buying the rest of the Aireon aircraft-tracking venture its connectivity constellation hosts to push further into aviation safety, surveillance and data services.

title: CEO Series: Chris Quilty looks at the future of the space industry
AST SpaceMobile may use ULA’s Vulcan
description: AST SpaceMobile may launch some of its direct-to-device satellites on United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan rocket to expand the launch options for its constellation.

title: House appropriators approve spending bill that keeps NASA budget flat
description: The House Appropriations Committee advanced a spending bill May 13 that rejects many of the cuts to NASA proposed by the administration.

title: Why Earth observation data is getting stuck in orbit
description: With the proliferation of Earth observation satellites in orbit, the increasing sophistication of sensors and surging demand for imagery and geospatial intelligence across a growing number of sectors, it is no surprise that more data is now collected than ever before. But what is perhaps surprising is that the main obstacle to the smooth operation […]

title: Landspace launches improved Zhuque-2E, Long March 6A lofts new Qianfan satellite group
description: China has added a new batch of satellites to the Qianfan megaconstellation, while the commercial Zhuque-2E made a return-to-flight featuring numerous improvements.

title: SLS to launch without upper stage for Artemis 3
description: NASA plans to fly the Space Launch System on Artemis 3 without an upper stage as the agency begins to define revised plans for the mission.

title: Rocketeers are Competing at the IREC for Your Attention

description: The LR-450 is designed for spacecraft operating in the absence of GPS signals

title: SES joins Eutelsat in canceling GEO expansion satellites
description: SES has canceled two satellites Intelsat ordered before being acquired by the Luxembourg-based multi-orbit operator, joining France’s Eutelsat in pulling back from GEO expansion plans drawn up just a few years ago.

title: SpaceX sets date for first Starship version 3 launch
description: SpaceX has set a date for the long-delayed first launch of its next-generation Starship vehicle, which is critical to the company’s ambitions as well as NASA’s lunar plans.

title: Varda to collaborate with United Therapeutics on microgravity drug research
description: Varda Space Industries has signed its first major agreement with a pharmaceutical company to develop improved drugs in microgravity.

title: FCC approves SpaceX spectrum deal with $2.4 billion escrow condition
description: SpaceX has the FCC’s blessing to buy EchoStar spectrum to improve direct-to-device services in the United States, subject to a $2.4 billion escrow tied to disputes over the seller’s abandoned terrestrial 5G network buildout.

title: US Army’s 7th Infantry Division, 1st MDTF to merge as Multi-Domain Command-Pacific
description: The two-star Multi-Domain Command-Pacific will merge the 7th ID’s Stryker infantry brigades and a combat aviation brigade with a multidomain task force.

title: US Army to receive thousands of Barracuda-500M cruise missiles in Anduril deal
description: The Pentagon’s Low-Cost Containerized Missiles program aims to obtain over 10,000 low-cost cruise missiles over a three-year span.

title: Army leaders in hot seat over Poland deployment cancellation
description: Lawmakers questioned the timing and the reasons, lambasting the order that Rep. Don Bacon, R-Neb., said sent a “terrible message to Russia and our allies.”

title: No sound of silence: US soldiers train eyes — and ears — for drone swarms
description: The U.S. Army is moving beyond battling individual drone threats as it experiments with tactics to combat throngs of unmanned aircraft in saturated skies.

title: USS Gerald R. Ford to return from 11-month deployment on Saturday
description: The world’s largest aircraft carrier is finally returning home after etching its name into Navy history books.

title: Canada-led brigade in Latvia moves beyond tripwire role, commander says
description: “Right now I have a brigade, there is nothing on the other side of the border that can take out this brigade,” the unit commander told Defense News.

title: US Marines practice seizing remote islands in Philippine exercise
description: Based on Oahu, MADIS crews face limitations when live-firing at home, so Konien was enthusiastic about the opportunity to fire rounds and missiles abroad.

title: DoD faces mounting pressure to pass clean audit for the first time
description: To date, the DoD has never passed a full, clean audit since they first became mandated in 2018.

title: Near Russian border, NATO grapples with ground robots in combat
description: “We are a little bit behind because we’ve been using only the air drones,” a Latvian commander said.

title: China fires verbal warning shot at US over Taiwan
description: Any misstep over Taiwan could push the U.S. and China toward direct confrontation, Chinese leader Xi Jinping told President Donald Trump.

title: Lawmakers push for domestic shipbuilding fixes as US Navy explores overseas options

title: Iran military threat is diminished but not eliminated, CENTCOM chief says
description: Adm. Brad Cooper said the U.S. had severely degraded Iran’s warfighting capacity, including the elimination of roughly 90% of its inventory of naval mines.

title: Blacklists, corruption and frontline needs: Ukraine tackles an arms-export puzzle

description: Elbit Systems was awarded the development contract, valued at approximately $34 million (100 million NIS).

title: Japan fires first-ever missiles from Philippine soil

title: US Army recovers remains of second soldier reported missing during Moroccan exercise
description: The Army identified the remains of Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, one of the two soldiers reported missing on May 2.

title: No Indo-Pacific peace without industry surge and burden sharing, US general says
description: Pushing industry to far-reaching frontlines — and having allies share the load — is vital to Indo-Pacific security, U.S. Army Gen. Xavier Brunson stressed.

title: US Navy could run out of money by July, top officer warns
description: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle told lawmakers at a budget hearing Tuesday that the Navy might have to modify training and operations.

title: Contractor awarded $3.5 billion to build out US Coast Guard’s Arctic Security Cutters fleet

title: US Army abruptly cancels deployment of 4,000 soldiers to Poland
description: The Army has abruptly halted a planned deployment to Poland, bringing U.S. personnel numbers in Europe to pre-2022 levels — before Russia invaded Ukraine.

title: Air Force MQ-9 fleet drops to 135 aircraft after Iran combat losses

title: Role of Indo-Pacific air defenders has evolved dramatically, US Army commander says

title: US special operations leaders frustrated by inability to modify their own equipment
description: The military must wait for manufacturers to fix or upgrade equipment, leaving them with outdated capabilities as technology evolves quickly, they said.
